---
title: "Automate The 3MT Presentation Schedule and Email"
id: 3mt_schedule_and_email
author: brad_cannell
date: 2026-02-18
date-modified: 2026-02-20
audience: self
domain: administration
tags:
  - 3MT
  - ADR
  - Scheduling
  - Data Cleaning
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    embed-resources: true
execute:
  eval: false
---

# Overview

- This is a template for generating the 3MT schedule and email to participants.
- 2025-02-20: This is all in Python because I originally expected to use AI. However, the code below doesn't actually ever call AI. If you don't eventually add AI, you might want to convert this to R.
- I got help with this from ChatGPT in the browser: https://chatgpt.com/g/g-p-6996201538d881918045948a02f806df/c/69962272-4aa0-8326-94b1-34014dac0a30

# Prerequisites
- Create a new virtual environment (uv).
    - `uv init`
- Install Packages
    - `uv add keyring openai jupyter -U chatlas`
- Load environment on a new machine
    - `uv sync`
    - - Select the virtual environment as the Python interpreter (Shift+Command+P).

# Load Packages

```{python}
from datetime import date, datetime, timedelta
from collections import defaultdict
from docx.oxml import OxmlElement
from docx.shared import Pt
from docx.oxml.ns import qn
from docx import Document
from openai import OpenAI
from pathlib import Path
import chatlas as ctl
import pandas as pd
import keyring
import heapq
import json
import math
import os
```

# File Paths and Environment Variables

They are passed to the AI assistant below.

```{python}
# OpenAI Models: https://platform.openai.com/docs/models
# Anthropic Models: https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/about-claude/models/overview
model = "gpt-5.2-2025-12-11"

# Today's date
today = date.today().isoformat()

# Event Details
event_date = "Friday, March 6, 2026"
event_time = "11:30 am – 1:30 pm"
event_location = "Bass Hall, Room 1004"

# What time should the first presentation start?
# Used in compute_presentation_spacing() and generate_start_times()
presentation_start_time = "11:35 AM"

# What time should the judging and awards start?
# Used in compute_presentation_spacing()
presentation_end_time = "12:23 PM"

# Path to the intent-to-participate data (Qualtrics)
qualtrics_path = Path('/Users/bradcannell/Library/CloudStorage/Box-Box/AI Assistant/conversations/ADR/3MT/Development/Qualtrics Data Raw.xlsx')

# Path to the 3MT schedule template
schedule_template_path = Path('/Users/bradcannell/Library/CloudStorage/Box-Box/AI Assistant/conversations/ADR/3MT/Development/3MT Schedule Template.docx')
```

# Import and Clean the Intent-to-Participate Data (Qualtrics)

```{python}
# Import the Qualtrics data
qualtrics = pd.read_excel(
    qualtrics_path,
    engine="openpyxl",
    header=0,      # First row = column names
    skiprows=[1]   # Skip the second row (labels)
)

# Select columns of interest
keep_cols = [
    "name_first", "name_last", "email", "department", "degree", "title", 
    "name_mentor",
]
qualtrics = qualtrics.loc[:, keep_cols]

# Drop students who no longer want to participate
qualtrics = qualtrics[qualtrics["name_first"] != "Paulina"]
```

## Convert the intent-to-participate data to JSON

- AI models behave much better with structured JSON than pasted tables.

```{python}
# Convert to a JSON list/dict object
qualtrics_json = qualtrics.to_dict(orient="records")
# Also create a string that looks like JSON
qualtrics_json_str = json.dumps(qualtrics_json, indent=2)
```

## Arrange presentations by department

Reorder contestants to avoid same-department back-to-back (if possible).

Goal: create an ordering such that adjacent contestants are not from the same department, when feasible.

This is basically a “rearrange string so no adjacent equal” problem. A reliable greedy approach:
    - Group contestants by department
    - Always pick the department with the most remaining contestants, unless it’s the same as the last one used
    - If the only remaining department is the same as last, you’re forced to place it (constraint not fully satisfiable)

```{python}
def reorder_to_avoid_adjacent_department(contestants, department_key="department"):
    """
    Reorder a list of contestant dicts to minimize adjacent contestants
    from the same department.

    Parameters
    ----------
    contestants : list[dict]
        Each dict must have department_key.
    department_key : str
        Key that identifies department.

    Returns
    -------
    reordered : list[dict]
    success : bool
        True if no adjacent same-department pairs exist in the result.
        False if constraint could not be fully satisfied.
    """
    # Bucket contestants by department
    buckets = defaultdict(list)
    for c in contestants:
        dept = (c.get(department_key) or "").strip()
        buckets[dept].append(c)

    # Max-heap by remaining count
    heap = []
    for dept, items in buckets.items():
        heapq.heappush(heap, (-len(items), dept))

    reordered = []
    last_dept = None

    while heap:
        count1, dept1 = heapq.heappop(heap)

        if dept1 != last_dept:
            # Use dept1
            item = buckets[dept1].pop(0)
            reordered.append(item)
            last_dept = dept1
            count1 += 1  # less negative

            if -count1 > 0:
                heapq.heappush(heap, (count1, dept1))
        else:
            # dept1 is same as last; try next best
            if not heap:
                # No alternative exists -> must place dept1 (constraint impossible)
                item = buckets[dept1].pop(0)
                reordered.append(item)
                last_dept = dept1
                count1 += 1
                if -count1 > 0:
                    heapq.heappush(heap, (count1, dept1))
            else:
                count2, dept2 = heapq.heappop(heap)

                # Place dept2 instead
                item = buckets[dept2].pop(0)
                reordered.append(item)
                last_dept = dept2
                count2 += 1

                # Push back remaining
                if -count2 > 0:
                    heapq.heappush(heap, (count2, dept2))
                heapq.heappush(heap, (count1, dept1))

    # Check success: no adjacent same department
    success = True
    for i in range(1, len(reordered)):
        d_prev = (reordered[i-1].get(department_key) or "").strip()
        d_curr = (reordered[i].get(department_key) or "").strip()
        if d_prev == d_curr:
            success = False
            break

    return reordered, success
```

This returns:
    - reordered_list
    - no_consecutive_depts (True if fully satisfied; False if unavoidable adjacency remains)

```{python}
qualtrics_json_reordered, no_consecutive_depts = reorder_to_avoid_adjacent_department(qualtrics_json, department_key="department")
no_consecutive_depts
```

## Compute presentation spacing.

Compute the “optimal” spacing from start/end time + number of contestants.

Interpretation: distribute presenters evenly across the available time window.
    - Inputs: n, start_time, end_time
    - Output: spacing_minutes (integer) + optionally the generated start times

This version:
    - Parses strings like "11:30 AM" / "1:30 PM"
    - Computes total available minutes
    - Uses floor(total / n) by default (safe; won’t exceed end time)
    - Returns both the spacing and a list of start time strings you can use directly

```{python}
def compute_presentation_spacing(
    n_contestants: int,
    start_time: str,
    end_time: str,
    time_fmt: str = "%I:%M %p",
    rounding: str = "floor",
):
    """
    Compute minutes per contestant given a window.

    Parameters
    ----------
    n_contestants : int
        Number of contestants (must be >= 1).
    start_time, end_time : str
        Time-of-day strings (e.g., "11:30 AM", "1:30 PM").
    time_fmt : str
        strptime format for times.
    rounding : {"floor","ceil","round"}
        How to convert raw spacing (float minutes) to integer.

    Returns
    -------
    spacing_minutes : int
    total_minutes : int
    raw_spacing : float
    """
    if n_contestants < 1:
        raise ValueError("n_contestants must be >= 1")

    t0 = datetime.strptime(start_time, time_fmt)
    t1 = datetime.strptime(end_time, time_fmt)

    # Handle windows that cross noon/midnight (rare for 3MT, but safe)
    if t1 <= t0:
        t1 = t1 + timedelta(days=1)

    total_minutes = int((t1 - t0).total_seconds() / 60)
    raw_spacing = total_minutes / n_contestants

    if rounding == "floor":
        spacing_minutes = max(1, math.floor(raw_spacing))
    elif rounding == "ceil":
        spacing_minutes = max(1, math.ceil(raw_spacing))
    elif rounding == "round":
        spacing_minutes = max(1, int(round(raw_spacing)))
    else:
        raise ValueError("rounding must be one of: 'floor', 'ceil', 'round'")

    return spacing_minutes, total_minutes, raw_spacing


def generate_start_times(
    n_contestants: int,
    event_start_time: str,
    spacing_minutes: int,
    input_fmt: str = "%I:%M %p",
    output_style: str = "lower_nospace",  # "11:35am"
):
    """
    Generate a list of start times for each contestant.
    """
    t0 = datetime.strptime(event_start_time, input_fmt)
    out = []
    for i in range(n_contestants):
        t = t0 + timedelta(minutes=spacing_minutes * i)
        if output_style == "lower_nospace":
            out.append(t.strftime("%I:%M%p").lstrip("0").lower())
        elif output_style == "ampm_space":
            out.append(t.strftime("%I:%M %p").lstrip("0"))
        else:
            raise ValueError("Unknown output_style.")
    return out
```

```{python}
n = len(qualtrics_json_reordered)
spacing_minutes, total, raw = compute_presentation_spacing(n, presentation_start_time, presentation_end_time)
presentation_start_times = generate_start_times(n, presentation_start_time, spacing_minutes)
spacing_minutes, presentation_start_times
```

# Add the Presentation Schedule to the Schedule Template

- Upadate the template Word document.
- This function will replace {{SCHEDULE_BLOCK}} with the presentation schedule.

🔴 Move the template paths in the chunk below to the File Paths and Environment Variables section. Also, update the file paths to the ADR folder. 

```{python}

DEV_DIR = Path("/Users/bradcannell/Library/CloudStorage/Box-Box/AI Assistant/conversations/ADR/3MT/Development")

template_path = DEV_DIR / "3MT Schedule Template.docx"
output_path   = DEV_DIR / "3MT_Schedule_2026_Filled.docx"

PLACEHOLDER = "{{SCHEDULE_BLOCK}}"
FONT_SIZE_PT = 12


def _insert_paragraph_after(paragraph):
    """Insert an empty paragraph after `paragraph` and return it."""
    new_p = OxmlElement("w:p")
    paragraph._p.addnext(new_p)
    new_para = paragraph._parent.add_paragraph("")  # temporary; we repoint XML next line
    new_para._p = new_p
    return new_para


def _set_spacing(paragraph, space_before=0, space_after=0, line_spacing=1.0):
    pf = paragraph.paragraph_format
    pf.space_before = Pt(space_before)
    pf.space_after = Pt(space_after)
    pf.line_spacing = line_spacing


def _format_time_lower(dt_obj):
    # e.g., "11:35am" / "12:07pm"
    return dt_obj.strftime("%I:%M%p").lstrip("0").lower()


def build_schedule_rows_from_qualtrics_with_times(qualtrics_json, start_times):
    """
    Build schedule rows using a precomputed list of start times (strings like '11:35am').

    qualtrics_json: list[dict] or JSON string
    start_times: list[str] with same length as qualtrics_json
    """
    import json
    if isinstance(qualtrics_json, str):
        qualtrics_json = json.loads(qualtrics_json)

    if len(start_times) != len(qualtrics_json):
        raise ValueError(
            f"start_times length ({len(start_times)}) must match contestants ({len(qualtrics_json)})."
        )

    rows = []
    for i, (rec, t) in enumerate(zip(qualtrics_json, start_times), start=1):
        first = (rec.get("name_first") or "").strip()
        last  = (rec.get("name_last") or "").strip()
        name = " ".join([p for p in [first, last] if p]).strip() or "[MISSING]"

        rows.append({
            "contestant_num": i,
            "start_time": (t or "[MISSING]").strip(),
            "name": name,
            "department": (rec.get("department") or "[MISSING]").strip(),
            "mentor": (rec.get("name_mentor") or "[MISSING]").strip(),
            "title": (rec.get("title") or "[MISSING]").strip(),
        })

    return rows


def _add_run(paragraph, text, *, bold=False, italic=False, font_size_pt=12):
    run = paragraph.add_run(text)
    run.bold = bold
    run.italic = italic
    run.font.size = Pt(font_size_pt)
    return run


def replace_all_schedule_placeholders(doc, placeholder, schedule_rows):
    """
    Replace ALL paragraphs containing placeholder text with the formatted schedule block.
    This avoids surprises if the placeholder exists more than once.
    """
    placeholder_paragraphs = [p for p in doc.paragraphs if placeholder in p.text]
    if not placeholder_paragraphs:
        raise ValueError(f"Placeholder {placeholder!r} not found in template.")

    for target in placeholder_paragraphs:
        # Clear placeholder paragraph completely
        target.text = ""
        _set_spacing(target, space_before=0, space_after=0, line_spacing=1.05)

        current = target

        for idx, r in enumerate(schedule_rows):
            # Line 1: time – Contestant N  (bold)
            p1 = current if idx == 0 else _insert_paragraph_after(current)
            _set_spacing(p1, space_before=0, space_after=0, line_spacing=1.05)
            _add_run(
                p1,
                f"{r['start_time']} – Contestant {r['contestant_num']}",
                bold=True,
                italic=False,
                font_size_pt=FONT_SIZE_PT,
            )

            # Line 2: Name (bold) – Department / Mentor (normal)
            p2 = _insert_paragraph_after(p1)
            _set_spacing(p2, space_before=0, space_after=0, line_spacing=1.05)
            _add_run(p2, f"{r['name']}", bold=True, italic=False, font_size_pt=FONT_SIZE_PT)
            _add_run(
                p2,
                f" – {r['department']} / {r['mentor']}",
                bold=False,
                italic=False,
                font_size_pt=FONT_SIZE_PT,
            )

            # Line 3: Title (italic)
            p3 = _insert_paragraph_after(p2)
            _set_spacing(p3, space_before=0, space_after=0, line_spacing=1.05)
            _add_run(p3, r["title"], bold=False, italic=True, font_size_pt=FONT_SIZE_PT)

            # Blank line between contestants
            p_blank = _insert_paragraph_after(p3)
            _set_spacing(p_blank, space_before=0, space_after=0, line_spacing=1.0)

            current = p_blank


# ---- Build schedule rows from your existing qualtrics_json ----
# Presentation_start_times was created in the previous chunk
schedule_rows = build_schedule_rows_from_qualtrics_with_times(
    qualtrics_json=qualtrics_json_reordered,      # IMPORTANT: use the reordered roster
    start_times=presentation_start_times
)

# ---- Fill Word template ----
doc = Document(template_path)

# Replace schedule placeholder(s)
replace_all_schedule_placeholders(doc, PLACEHOLDER, schedule_rows)

doc.save(output_path)
output_path
```

# Import and Clean Confirmation Email Template

🔴 Move the template paths in the chunk below to the File Paths and Environment Variables section. Also, update the file paths to the ADR folder. 

```{python}
def replace_tokens_in_paragraph(paragraph, token_map):
    """
    Replace tokens in a paragraph while trying to preserve formatting.
    Works by editing runs. If a token is split across runs, we fall back
    to a paragraph-level replace (which can flatten formatting).
    """
    # Fast path: if no tokens present, do nothing
    full_text = paragraph.text
    if not any(tok in full_text for tok in token_map.keys()):
        return

    # Try run-level replacement first (preserves formatting better)
    for run in paragraph.runs:
        for tok, val in token_map.items():
            if tok in run.text:
                run.text = run.text.replace(tok, str(val))

    # If tokens were split across runs, run-level replacement may miss them.
    # Detect remaining tokens and do a paragraph-level replace as a fallback.
    updated_text = paragraph.text
    if any(tok in updated_text for tok in token_map.keys()):
        # This fallback can lose mixed formatting within the paragraph,
        # but it ensures correctness.
        for tok, val in token_map.items():
            updated_text = updated_text.replace(tok, str(val))
        paragraph.text = updated_text


def replace_tokens_in_docx(doc, token_map):
    # Replace in normal paragraphs
    for p in doc.paragraphs:
        replace_tokens_in_paragraph(p, token_map)

    # Replace in tables (cells contain paragraphs)
    for table in doc.tables:
        for row in table.rows:
            for cell in row.cells:
                for p in cell.paragraphs:
                    replace_tokens_in_paragraph(p, token_map)


# ---- Paths (edit these) ----
template_path = Path('/Users/bradcannell/Library/CloudStorage/Box-Box/AI Assistant/conversations/ADR/3MT/Development/3MT_Email_Template.docx')

output_path = Path('/Users/bradcannell/Library/CloudStorage/Box-Box/AI Assistant/conversations/ADR/3MT/Development/3MT_Email_2026_Filled.docx')
# ---- Token values (edit these) ----
token_map = {
    "{{EVENT_DATE}}": "Friday, March 6, 2026",
    "{{EVENT_TIME}}": "11:30 AM–1:30 PM",
    "{{EVENT_LOCATION}}": "Bass Hall, Room 1004",
    "{{ARRIVAL_TIME}}": "11:15 AM",
    "{{CONTACT_NAME}}": "Eva Leon",
    "{{CORRECTION_DEADLINE}}": "Friday, February 27, 2026",
    "{{SENDER_NAME}}": "Brad Cannell",
    "{{SENDER_TITLE}}": "Associate Dean for Research",
}

# ---- Fill template ----
doc = Document(template_path)
replace_tokens_in_docx(doc, token_map)
doc.save(output_path)

output_path
```

Quick verification. If it returns [], you’re good.

```{python}
doc_check = Document(output_path)
all_text = "\n".join(p.text for p in doc_check.paragraphs)
missing = [tok for tok in token_map.keys() if tok in all_text]
missing
```
